page: Vultr vbm-8c-64gb-amd is a bare metal server powered by an AMD EPYC 4345P processor built on the x86_64 architecture. This server features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, supported by 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a local 3890 GB NVMe SSD, and the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU hardware is present in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, scoring in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Passmark memory testing also ranks in the top tier. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core CPU and Passmark CPU scores, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth read speeds are average, though large-block uncached write bandwidth is a relative bottleneck, placing in the bottom 25%. Small-model LLM inference is highly efficient, with text generation for a 135M model reaching top-tier speeds.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal server offers high resource density by combining dedicated, non-virtualized AMD EPYC CPU resources with a substantial 3890 GB local NVMe SSD. This makes it a strong candidate for workloads requiring direct hardware access without hypervisor overhead. The server is well-suited for database hosting, local storage-intensive applications, web serving, and single-threaded processing tasks.
description: Vultr vbm-8c-64gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring an AMD EPYC 4345P processor with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM and a substantial 3890 GB local NVMe SSD. The x86_64 architecture delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, single-threaded compression, and memory scores, while multi-core operations and database processing perform at average levels. Uncached large-block memory write bandwidth represents a performance tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25%. Lacking a GPU, this server provides high resource density and direct hardware access without virtualization overhead. It is highly suitable for storage-heavy applications, database hosting, static web serving, and single-threaded processing workloads.
og_description: Vultr vbm-8c-64gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring a 16-core AMD EPYC 4345P CPU, 64 GB RAM, and 3890 GB NVMe storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for databases and storage-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-8c-64gb-amd is a bare metal server with a 16-core AMD EPYC CPU, 64 GB RAM, and 3890 GB NVMe storage, optimized for databases and web serving.
tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining high-speed single-core AMD processing with massive local NVMe storage for database and storage-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 4345P processor with 16 dedicated physical cores at 3.9 GHz
Generous 3890 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
64.0 GB of system memory delivering top-tier Passmark memory scores
Ideal for database hosting, static web serving, and storage-heavy applications
